https://bugzilla.lustre.org/show_bug.cgi?id=24536#c8

Patch Set 1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

According to your comment on LU-618, this patch cannot be landed.
I think we can bypass the function definition issue through re-implement the function by Lustre, although it is some hack, it maks Lustre runnable.

Topic set to "Abandon".

This patch is a candidate to be abandoned because it has been idle for a long time, or may be obsoleted by another patch that has already been landed.  If you think this patch still contains useful changes, please comment in the patch.
Topic "Abandon" removed.

parents.subject: LU-10268 lfsck: postpone lfsck start until initialized

commit.subject: LU-618 llite: IO accounting of page read
commit.message:
LU-618 llite: IO accounting of page read

When CONFIG_TASK_IO_ACCOUNTING is used with Lustre, writes are
accounted for but not read.

The accounting is normally done in the kernel for page writeback
and readahead functionlity, Therefore, as Lustre implements its
own readahead, it must also maintain its own accounting on read
(but not for write)
